completion_promises.criterion_text: src/jarvis/tools/general.py exposes read_file(path: str) -> str decorated with @tool(parse_docstring=True).
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented read_file in src/jarvis/tools/general.py with @tool(parse_docstring=True) decorator. Verified via TestAC001ToolSurface (4 tests: BaseTool isinstance, name='read_file', args_schema with 'path' property, parse_docstring-derived description present).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Docstring matches API-tools.md §1.1 byte-for-byte (it IS the contract per DDR-005 precedent).
completion_promises.evidence: Docstring copied verbatim from docs/design/FEAT-JARVIS-002/contracts/API-tools.md §1.1. Verified via TestAC002DocstringContract (first sentence match, all 5 error categories listed, 'No network I/O.' clause present).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Resolves path relative to config.workspace_root; rejects paths whose os.path.realpath resolves outside workspace with ERROR: path_traversal — path resolves outside workspace: <resolved>.
completion_promises.evidence: _resolve_workspace_root() reads JarvisConfig().workspace_root. Workspace-relative paths joined onto root; os.path.realpath() resolves to canonical form; _is_inside() check via Path.is_relative_to. Returns 'ERROR: path_traversal — path resolves outside workspace: <resolved>'. Verified via TestAC003HappyPath (4 tests: relative read, absolute inside-workspace read, nested subdir, unicode round-trip) and TestAC004PathTraversalRejected (../ relative + absolute outside).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Rejects paths containing embedded null bytes (\x00) with ERROR: path_traversal — ... (ASSUM-003: same category, no new one).
completion_promises.evidence: Null-byte check is the first guard in read_file body, before any os.* call (Python's open() raises ValueError on \x00 paths). Returns 'ERROR: path_traversal — path contains embedded null byte'. Verified via TestAC004PathTraversalRejected.test_embedded_null_byte_rejected_as_path_traversal.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Rejects symlinks whose resolved target lies outside workspace with ERROR: path_traversal — ... (ASSUM-002).
completion_promises.evidence: os.path.realpath() resolves symlinks before the inside-workspace check, so a symlink whose target lies outside is rejected through the same code path as ../-style traversal. Verified via TestAC005SymlinkEscapeRejected (creates symlink in workspace pointing to file outside; asserts ERROR: path_traversal). Includes a graceful skip if filesystem rejects symlinks.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Returns ERROR: not_found — ... for non-existent paths; ERROR: not_a_file — ... for directories; ERROR: too_large — ... for files > 1 MiB (boundary: exactly 1 MiB = accept; 1 MiB + 1 byte = reject); ERROR: encoding — ... for non-UTF-8 bytes.
completion_promises.evidence: MAX_FILE_BYTES = 1024*1024. Sequence: exists → is_file → size <= MAX → utf-8 decode. Verified via TestAC006StructuredErrors (5 tests: not_found, not_a_file for directory, exactly-1MiB accepted, 1MiB+1 rejected with 'exceeds 1MB', invalid UTF-8 → encoding error).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Never raises an exception; all internal errors are caught and converted to structured strings per ADR-ARCH-021.
completion_promises.evidence: Outer try wraps the entire body. Specific (PermissionError, OSError) handler returns ERROR: not_found. Bare 'except Exception' catch-all calls logger.exception() then returns 'ERROR: not_found — read_file failed: ...'. Verified via TestAC007NeverRaises (pathological inputs return strings; monkeypatch _resolve_workspace_root to raise RuntimeError → returns ERROR: string).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Seam test: calling read_file inside assemble_tool_list-wired supervisor produces the structured error string, not a raised exception (end-to-end through the @tool wrapper).
completion_promises.evidence: TestAC008SeamThroughToolWrapper invokes read_file.invoke({'path': ...}) — the same BaseTool.invoke API that assemble_tool_list (TASK-J002-015, Wave 3) will route through. Three tests: missing file → ERROR: not_found string (no raise); outside-workspace absolute path → ERROR: path_traversal string (no raise); valid file → contents returned. assemble_tool_list itself is delivered in TASK-J002-015 (Wave 3) so the seam tested here is the @tool wrapper, which is the only seam present in this dependency wave.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: tests/test_tools_general.py passes 'python -m ruff check' clean. src/jarvis/tools/general.py was edited by another concurrent task to include calculate/get_calendar_events/search_web tooling — those additional sections are outside TASK-J002-008 scope. The read_file function and its supporting helpers (_resolve_workspace_root, _is_inside, MAX_FILE_BYTES) added by this task are lint-clean.
